data.title: Interchain Labs CEO Expresses Sympathy Over OM Incident
data.description: According to Foresight News, Interchain Labs Co-CEO Magmar has expressed deep sympathy for those affected by the OM incident. In a recent tweet, Magmar stated, "I am shocked, but I will continue to support the community as much as I can. I am here to provide resources for all OMies."

data.title: Metaplanet Acquires 319 More Bitcoin as Holdings Climb to 4,525 BTC
data.description:
Metaplanet Inc. has acquired 319 more BTC, raising its total holdings to 4,525 BTC. With a 2025 BTC Yield of 108. 3%, the Japan-based firm deepens its Bitcoin-centric treasury strategy.

The post Metaplanet Acquires 319 More Bitcoin as Holdings Climb to 4,525 BTC first appeared on Ecoinimist.

source: https://ecoinimist.com/2025/04/14/metaplanet-acquires-more-bitcoin/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=metaplanet-acquires-more-bitcoin

data.title: OKX CEO Star: OM flash crash is a major scandal, ready to release a full report
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News OKX CEO Star posted on the X platform, "OM's flash crash is a major scandal for the entire crypto industry. All unlocking and recharge data on the chain are public, and the mortgage and liquidation data of major trading platforms can also be verified. OKX will prepare all relevant reports."

data.title: Gate.io Launches KERNEL Spot and Contract Trading
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, Gate.io will launch KERNEL spot trading at 20:00 (UTC+8) on April 14, and simultaneously open KERNEL perpetual contract real trading (USDT settlement), supporting 1-50 times leverage.

data.title: Bitget responds to campus ambassador controversy: always stick to the bottom line and will launch the "Green Campus Plan"
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Bitget Chinese head Xie Jiayin released a clarification and explanation about the campus ambassador program on social media, saying, "In response to BlockBeats' call for major trading platforms to stop promoting contract experience money to college students, Bitget made the following explanation: 
1. About the campus ambassador program: Bitget's campus ambassador program aims to promote blockchain knowledge and provide internship opportunities, and does not encourage contract trading or issue contract experience money. The program was launched on February 18. Because users misunderstood that it was related to contract rebates, Bitget was quickly offline worldwide on the morning of February 19, and all applications during the period were rejected. 
2. Adhere to principles and refuse to induce: Bitget has never distributed contract experience money to students in any form. This is Bitget's bottom line. Social supervision is welcome. If such behavior is found, screenshots can be reported. Bitget will deal with it seriously and give effective reporters up to 1,000 USDT rewards. 
3. University cooperation is limited to popular science and employment: Bitget's cooperation with universities focuses on Web3 popular science, internships and employment guidance, such as 2025 Campus recruitment (28 people have been hired), Zhejiang University hackathon, HKUST Web3 sharing session, etc.
Bitget has always supported industry self-discipline and actively responded to the BlockBeats initiative. On this basis, Bitget will launch the "Green Campus Plan" to help college students understand Web3 and explore the future in a healthier way. Everyone is welcome to supervise and make suggestions. ”

data.title: OM Token's 90% Crash Leaves Holders Reeling —MANTRA Blames 'Reckless Liquidations,' CEO Says 'Fixing This'
data.description:
The native cryptocurrency of a real-world asset-focused project dramatically crashed by nearly 90% on Sunday, wiping out billions of dollars worth of investors' fortunes. 
source: https://www.benzinga.com/25/04/44781876/om-tokens-90-crash-leaves-holders-reeling-with-lossesmantra-blames-reckless-liquidations-but-are-big-players-involved?utm_source=cryptopanic.com&utm_campaign=partner_feed&utm_medium=partner_feed&utm_content=site
